km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Cadwgan和Angharad verch Llewelyn至少有1個女兒。他去世於1403年之後在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Cadwgan和Angharad verch Llewelyn至少有1个女儿。他去世于1403年之后在英国。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Long, Minh, Muoi, Phan, Phat, Vinh, Cuong, Hien, Hoa, Lam, Lan.  Chinese Wing, Chi, Kin, Cheuk, Chi Wai, Chuen, Hon, Keung, Wah, Wai, Chiu, Ho.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 敖, see   Ao  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Auerbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Emanuel, Isidor, Bronia, Chaim, Faigy, Menachem, Meyer, Yechezkel.
German: habitational name from any of several places in southern Germany and Austria called Auerbach ‘the stream at the water meadow’. See also 2,   Au  , and   Bach  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Auerbach in the Upper Palatinate, Bavaria. Compare   Averbach  .

variants.name: Ao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 敖, meaning both ‘rambling’ and ‘deposed king without posthumous titles’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Tai Ao (太敖), teacher of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437 BC ). (ii) it is said that the surname is also borne by descendants of deposed kings of the former state of Chu, with no posthumous titles, during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Auer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hans, Erwin, Matthias, Siegfried, Bernd, Bernhard, Erna, Franz, Fritz, Hermann, Horst.

German (mainly Bavaria): topographic name for someone living by a water meadow, German Au, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Au or Aue (see Au and Aue ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Slovenia.

nameMeanings.name: Cadwgan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cadogan
meanings: Welsh (Pembrokeshire and Glamorgan) and Irish (of Welsh origin): from the Old Welsh personal name Catgucaun, Catguocawn, modernized as Cadwgon. It is composed of cad ‘battle’ + gogawn ‘honour, glory’. See also Wogan . The surname is stressed, like its Welsh original, on the penultimate syllable, hence its occasional development to Duggan .

nameMeanings.name: Gruffudd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Griffudd
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Gruffud
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Cadwgan had at least 1 daughter with Angharad verch Llewelyn. He died after 1403, in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Cadwgan a eu au moins 1 fille avec Angharad verch Llewelyn. Il est décédé après 1403, à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Cadwgan hatte mit Angharad verch Llewelyn mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb nach 1403 in England.

it.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Cadwgan lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Angharad verch Llewelyn. Morì dopo il 1403, nel England.

ja.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap CadwganはAngharad verch Llewelynとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1403年以後にイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Cadwgan(은)는 Angharad verch Llewelyn(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1403년 이후 영국에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Cadwgan teve pelo menos 1 filha com Angharad verch Llewelyn. Ele faleceu após 1403, em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: У Gruffudd ap Cadwgan была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Angharad verch Llewelyn. Он умер после 1403 г. в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Gruffudd ap Cadwgan tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Angharad verch Llewelyn. Murió después de 1403, en Inglaterra.
